contexts.description: The `/dashboard/agents` sub-page showing the fleet of active AI personas: their status, cost, execution counts, and per-agent detail views. AgentMetrics renders spark-lines and stat badges per persona. FleetOptimizationCard surfaces AI-generated recommendations for reducing cost or improving throughput.
contexts.name: agents-module

contexts.description: Reusable animation primitives, hooks, and gating infrastructure. `animations.ts` exports framer-motion variants and transition presets. Animation hooks manage numbers tweening, auto-cycling, canvas compositing, and section-based pause. `AnimationPauseObserver` pauses expensive animations when sections leave the viewport. All GPU-intensive animations gate on `useReducedMotion` per the project lint rule.
contexts.name: animation-system

contexts.description: Client-side authentication state management for the demo dashboard. `authStore` (Zustand) holds the signed-in user, session token, and auth status. `AuthProvider` bootstraps the store on mount and subscribes to Supabase auth events. `mockAuth.ts` provides a deterministic fake auth flow for the demo environment when Supabase is not configured.
contexts.name: auth-system

contexts.description: The marketing blog at `/blog` featuring an RSS feed, category filtering, featured post highlight, and dynamic article pages with OG image generation. Blog post metadata lives in `src/data/blog.ts`. Articles are rendered by `BlogArticle.tsx` with shared markdown styling. The RSS feed route generates valid Atom XML.
contexts.name: blog

contexts.description: The visual identity foundation: brand color palette, semantic Tailwind token definitions, CSS custom properties for themes, and typography scale. `brand-theme.ts` exports BRAND_VAR constants used by SVG illustrations and animation components. `themes.css` and `tokens.css` define light/dark CSS variables consumed by Tailwind's design tokens.
contexts.name: brand-tokens

contexts.description: Build-time configuration and development tooling: Next.js config, TypeScript config, PostCSS, ESLint (including two custom rules for animation gating and low-opacity text), Playwright test runner config, and Sentry source-map upload integration. Custom ESLint rules under `eslint-rules/` enforce project-specific conventions.
contexts.name: build-config

contexts.description: A scrollable timeline of product release notes embedded in the marketing site. Changelog entries are stored in `src/data/changelog.ts` and rendered by the `changelog-timeline` section components. The PreviewRegistry at `/preview` also references changelog for component development.
contexts.name: changelog

contexts.description: The `/connections` page displaying the catalog of third-party integrations and connectors available to the Personas platform. Users can browse connector categories, open a modal for connector details, and see integration compatibility. Connector data is sourced from a static manifest (`src/data/connectors.ts`) and the display is generated from `src/lib/connectors.ts`.
contexts.name: connections-page

contexts.description: Remaining dashboard sub-pages that don't warrant their own contexts: Home overview (`/dashboard/home`), Messages thread view (`/dashboard/messages`), SLA tracking (`/dashboard/sla`), Leaderboard (`/dashboard/leaderboard`), and Settings (`/dashboard/settings`). Each is a focused single-page view built on the shared mock data layer and dashboard shell.
contexts.name: dashboard-misc-pages

contexts.description: The demo dashboard's persistent shell: layout, sidebar navigation, scope bar, authentication guard, and skeleton loader. The dashboard runs entirely on mock data from `src/lib/mockApi.ts`; AuthGuard redirects unauthenticated visitors to the sign-in prompt. DashboardScopeBar provides a date-range and agent-scope selector used by all sub-pages.
contexts.name: dashboard-shell

contexts.description: The `/how` and `/demo` marketing pages plus the download CTA section that drives app installs. The download API route at `/api/download` serves asset links for macOS, Windows, and Linux. The get-started and FAQ sections appear across multiple marketing pages to reduce friction.
contexts.name: download-how-pages

contexts.description: Playwright end-to-end test suite covering 13 user-facing flows: blog navigation, community, connection catalog, cookie consent, download, guide (10KB), legal, playground, roadmap voting, templates, guided tour (13KB), and use-cases. Tests run against the live dev server and verify real user interactions rather than mocked unit behavior.
contexts.name: e2e-tests

contexts.description: The `/dashboard/events` sub-page providing a real-time event bus visualizer, swimlane view, and filterable event list. Events stream via SSE from `/api/events/stream`. The swimlane layout renders event topology as parallel lanes. EventDetailDrawer shows full payload and tracing context per event.
contexts.name: events-module

contexts.description: The `/dashboard/executions` sub-page tracking individual agent execution runs: duration, cost, status, and streaming logs. Executions can be polled in real-time via `/api/executions/[id]/stream`. ExecChartWithCompare renders period-over-period comparison charts. The execution store caches run state client-side.
contexts.name: executions-module

contexts.description: The cinematic animated feature sections used on the home page and `/features` page: event bus showcase, team canvas, companion section, and the section-level lazy loading wrapper. These are showcase-only (not feature-page specific), providing dramatic visual proof points for the platform capabilities. `SectionWrapper` drives scroll-triggered reveal for all sections.
contexts.name: feature-showcase

contexts.description: The `/features` marketing page that showcases individual product capabilities through animated feature sections (Memory, Healing Circuit, Design Engine, Security Vault, Trigger System, Observability Deck, Plugins, Multi-Provider AI). Each section is an isolated animated component lazy-loaded on scroll. This page drives top-of-funnel awareness of the platform's technical differentiators.
contexts.name: features-page

contexts.description: A node-graph drag-and-drop visual composer for building agent pipelines, used within the playground. Nodes represent agents/tools; edges represent data flow. State is managed by `use-flow-composer.ts`. The composer supports adding, connecting, and configuring nodes via a panel component. Renders inside `FlowComposer.tsx`.
contexts.name: flow-composer

contexts.description: A full-featured knowledge base at `/guide` organized into categories and topics, with markdown rendering, table of contents, reading progress, search, and mobile-responsive layout. Guide content is stored as structured TypeScript data in `src/data/guide/` with 13-locale translations. `GuideMarkdown` renders MDX-like blocks via `GuideBlocks`. Search is handled client-side by `guide-search.ts`.
contexts.name: guide-system

contexts.description: An interactive guided product tour overlaid on the marketing site, walking new visitors through Personas' core value propositions via spotlight animations, caption cards, avatar countdown, and optional audio narration. Tour state is managed in `TourContext`. Six hooks coordinate scroll, keyboard navigation, spotlight sequencing, audio playback, and volume. Tour script (~14KB) defines the step sequence.
contexts.name: guided-tour

contexts.description: The `/dashboard/health` and `/dashboard/incidents` sub-pages surfacing system health scores, active incidents, and degradation history. HealthDigestPanel renders an AI-generated summary of current issues. HealthIssueRow displays individual incident cards with severity, affected agents, and status. Health scores are computed from mock data.
contexts.name: health-incidents-module

contexts.description: The marketing home page at `/` including the hero section, primary CTA, use-case highlights, vision grid, orchestration hub, and structured JSON-LD data for search engines. Hero content is split into a server component and a client-animated HeroClient to allow static rendering with progressive enhancement. Cross-links to the download CTA and features sections.
contexts.name: home-page

contexts.description: A custom 14-locale internationalization system with a static bundle approach: all translations are TypeScript files compiled into the bundle. `useTranslation()` reads the active locale from `i18nStore` and returns a typed `Translations` object. `LanguageSwitcher` persists the chosen locale. Dev/QA locale switching is gated behind an env flag. `tsc` enforces key completeness across all 14 locales.
contexts.name: i18n-system

contexts.description: The `/dashboard/knowledge` sub-page managing the agent memory system: semantic memories, pattern clusters, and recalled context. MemoryActionsPanel lets users pin, archive, or promote memory entries. The three sub-views (memories, patterns, clusters) reflect the knowledge graph structure of the orchestration platform.
contexts.name: knowledge-module

contexts.description: Static informational pages covering legal policies (privacy policy, terms of service, cookie policy) and the security documentation page. Policy content tracks changes via `policy-changelog.ts`. The security page displays compliance certifications, threat model, and disclosure process sourced from `security.ts`.
contexts.name: legal-security-pages

contexts.description: Mobile-specific UI components: MobileAppBar (top header), MobileBottomNav (tab bar), MobileShell (page wrapper), MobileSheet (bottom drawer), MobileStatCard (compact KPI card), MobileTabBar, MobileThreadSheet, and ViewFullSiteLink. These components share the same mock data layer as the desktop dashboard but are tuned for touch interaction and small viewports.
contexts.name: mobile-components

contexts.description: Mobile-optimized dashboard routes under `/m/*` providing lightweight views of alerts, messages, overview, and reviews. The mobile layout uses a bottom tab bar and app bar instead of the sidebar. A `ViewFullSiteLink` offers an escape hatch to the full desktop dashboard. Routes are separate from `/dashboard/*` to allow independent mobile optimization.
contexts.name: mobile-routes

contexts.api_surface: Internal mock — mirrors orchestrator REST API
contexts.description: The complete mock data and API layer that powers the demo dashboard without a real backend. `mockApi.ts` exposes typed async functions mimicking a REST/WebSocket orchestrator. `mock-dashboard-data.ts` contains seeded time-series generators for costs, executions, success rates, and agent metrics. `dashboard-queries.ts` wraps these in SWR-compatible fetchers.
contexts.name: mock-data-layer

contexts.description: Error tracking and analytics infrastructure. Sentry is configured with a custom PII scrubber (`sentry-pii.ts`) that strips UUIDs, URLs, quoted names, and sensitive breadcrumb fields before any event is sent. `analytics.ts` provides lightweight page-event tracking. `PageViewTracker` fires analytics on route changes. `instrumentation.ts` wires Sentry into the Next.js server runtime.
contexts.name: observability-infra

contexts.description: The `/dashboard/observability` sub-page with three views — activity, performance, and usage — showing cost trends, latency, traffic patterns, and spend distribution. UsageCharts, CostChartWithCompare, LatencyChart, and TrafficChart are all Recharts-based with a shared chart theme. `useLiveStats` polls mock data and `useSyncedRealtime` keeps multiple clients in sync.
contexts.name: observability-module

contexts.description: The interactive agent playground at `/playground` where visitors can send messages to a simulated multi-agent pipeline and see responses in real time. The split-pane layout shows a chat interface alongside an execution timeline. `agents-chat` and `playground-split` sections build the core interaction surface. The playground timeline visualizes the agent invocation chain.
contexts.name: playground

contexts.description: The `/dashboard/reviews` sub-page for human-in-the-loop review workflows: focus flow (single-item), split pane (compare), and keyboard shortcuts mode. Supports bulk accept/reject via `useReviewBulkActions` with undo toast. Optional voice narration of review items is managed by `reviewVoiceStore` and `useReviewVoice`, backed by a 36KB pre-generated voice data file.
contexts.name: reviews-module

contexts.description: The public product roadmap at `/roadmap` where users can view planned features and vote on priorities. Feature requests are stored in Supabase; votes are rate-limited per IP. API routes handle CRUD for requests, comments, votes, and a live roadmap JSON endpoint consumed by the desktop app. Roadmap phases data in `src/data/roadmap-phases.ts` drives the timeline display.
contexts.name: roadmap-voting

contexts.description: Shared server-side and isomorphic utilities: environment variable validation (`env.ts`), application constants, URL construction helpers, form/data validation, and the OG image generation utilities. `bodyScrollLock.ts` prevents background scroll when modals are open. `format.ts` and `format-date.ts` provide locale-aware display formatting.
contexts.name: server-utilities

contexts.description: The root Next.js layout, global error boundaries, SEO infrastructure, and top navigation bar that wrap every page on the site. The Navbar component contains desktop nav, mobile panel, download modal, and route definitions. It reads the theme store and i18n translations to render locale-aware navigation links.
contexts.name: site-shell

contexts.description: The `/api/stats` endpoint and `/api/personas/storage` + `/api/personas/publish` routes that surface aggregate usage statistics (installs, active users) and allow persona configuration publishing. Used by marketing pages to show live adoption numbers and by the desktop app to publish/sync persona configs.
contexts.name: stats-api

contexts.description: The Supabase integration layer: client initialization with anon-key-only access, typed API wrappers for roadmap/voting/waitlist tables, and server-side JSON file store fallback for environments without Supabase. All calls are guarded by URL+key presence checks to degrade gracefully. Rate limiting and request utilities support API routes.
contexts.name: supabase-layer

contexts.description: The `/templates` page providing a searchable catalog of pre-built agent pipeline templates users can inspect and clone. Template detail pages at `/templates/[id]` show full configuration, required connections, and setup steps. Template data (~63KB) is stored in `src/lib/templates.ts` and queried via `template-queries.ts`.
contexts.name: templates-library

contexts.description: Runtime light/dark/system theme switching. `themeStore` persists the user's preference to localStorage and applies a `data-theme` attribute to `<html>`. `ThemeInit` is a script injected into `<head>` to prevent flash-of-unstyled-content by reading localStorage synchronously before React hydrates. `ThemeSwitcher` renders the toggle UI.
contexts.name: theme-system

contexts.description: The shared component library of reusable UI building blocks: BrandCard, SectionIntro, TerminalPanel (with blinking cursor and history), ThemedChip, GlowCard, GradientText, RoleSelector, SVGFocusRing, LazyMount, and ShortcutHint. These are consumed across marketing sections, dashboard, and guide pages and adhere to the semantic Tailwind token conventions.
contexts.name: ui-primitives

contexts.description: The waitlist signup modal and API route that captures early-access email addresses. `WaitlistModal` renders a multi-step animated flow: form → loading → success panel with animated checkmark. Submissions are stored via the `/api/waitlist` route (Supabase). The modal is triggered globally from multiple CTAs.
contexts.name: waitlist
